title: Add Server-Side Pendo Activation Event
description:
Extend the existing user activation function (which sends a server-side event to Zapier) to also send an "Activated" track event to Pendo via their Track Events API. This enables tracking when a user activates directly within Pendo analytics.

The event uses a shared secret key (distinct from the subscription key) and sends a payload including visitor ID, account ID, timestamp, plan, IP address, URL, and page title. Activation is defined as a user adding their first object, which is distinct from registration (first sign-in).

Events must only fire in the production environment to maintain analytics integrity. Dev and staging servers must not send events.
acceptance_criteria: Activation event is sent to Pendo's Track Events API when a user activates
Event payload includes type, event name, visitor ID, account ID, timestamp, plan, IP, URL, and title
Pendo Track Event shared secret is securely stored
Events only fire in the production environment
Dev and staging servers do not send activation events
Activation event (first object added) is distinct from registration event (first sign-in)
Event appears in the Pendo analytics dashboard
middleware_changes: Extended the activation handler to send a server-side HTTP POST to Pendo's Track Events API alongside the existing Zapier webhook. The activation event fires when a user adds their first object.
api_changes: Outbound POST to Pendo Track Events endpoint with payload: {type: 'track', event: 'Activated', visitorId, accountId, timestamp, properties: {plan}, context: {ip, url, title}}.
implementation_notes: The Pendo Track Event shared secret is stored securely and is different from the Pendo subscription/installation key. Environment gating ensures only production fires events. The existing Zapier activation webhook at hooks.zapier.com/hooks/catch/2202974/31imef1/ continues to fire alongside the new Pendo event.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the Pendo activation event on production only, tested with a new Salesforce user registration and verified in the Pendo dashboard. Ryan clarified that 'registration' fires on first sign-in while 'activation' fires when a user adds their first object. Waqar confirmed both events are already implemented correctly.
